reviews.body:
**Actionable comments posted: 6** > [!CAUTION] > Some comments are outside the diff and can’t be posted inline due to platform limitations. > > > >
> ⚠️ Outside diff range comments (1)
> >
> src/services/planner.ts (1)
> > `131-133`: _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🔴 Critical_ > > **`auditReport` kan vara `undefined` — saknar null-guard.** > > `test_error.txt` dokumenterar exakt detta krasch-scenario: `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'highestPriorityGap')` vid rad 131. Om `runFullAudit` returnerar `undefined` (t.ex. vid fel eller om ingen data finns) kraschar koden här. > > > >
> 🐛 Föreslagen fix — lägg till null-guard > > ```diff > - if (auditReport.highestPriorityGap) { > + if (auditReport?.highestPriorityGap) { > ttb.lowest = "strength"; // Weakness found > } > ``` >
> >
> >
🤖 Fix all issues with AI agents ``` In @.agent/workflows/completed/night-shift-20260216.md: - Line 7: The frontmatter version ("version: \"2.5.0\"") and the document title ("Night Shift v2.3") are inconsistent; update one so they match—either change the title text to "Night Shift v2.5" to match the frontmatter or change the frontmatter version to "2.3.0" to match the title—ensure the change is applied wherever the title appears (document header) and in the frontmatter version field so both are identical. - Around line 305-312: The current cleanup loop aggressively deletes all local branches except main and night-shift using the STALE_BRANCHES variable and git branch -D; change it to only target branches that are already merged or otherwise safe by populating STALE_BRANCHES using git branch --merged (or filtering out branches without an upstream), and replace the hard delete git branch -D with a safer command (git branch -d) or prompt/confirm before deletion; ensure excluded patterns (main, night-shift) remain filtered and consider adding an explicit safelist array to skip important branches. In @.github/workflows/autonomous-trigger.yml: - Around line 68-71: The workflow currently exposes the full DATABASE_URL (and other secrets) to an AI-agent job that runs Gemini CLI with the --yolo flag, which lets the agent execute DB operations without review; remove DATABASE_URL from the environment for any job or step that runs with --yolo, replace it with a read-only credential variable (e.g., DATABASE_READONLY_URL) or conditionalize the env block so only non-YOLO runs get the full secret, and ensure the job that invokes Gemini/--yolo only receives limited secrets (refer to DATABASE_URL, DATABASE_READONLY_URL, NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL and the use of --yolo in the CLI invocation to locate and update the YAML). In `@package.json`: - Line 161: package.json currently lists "glob": "^11.0.1" which may resolve to 11.1.x that requires Node.js 20 or >=22; update the project to ensure compatibility by either (A) forcing a safe glob version (pin "glob" to "11.0.1" or add a package manager "overrides"/"resolutions" entry for "glob": "11.0.1") or (B) declaring and enforcing Node versions >=20 (and avoid Node 21) across environments by adding an "engines" field in package.json and updating CI/dev configs (.nvmrc, Dockerfile, GitHub Actions, etc.) to use Node 20 or >=22; pick one approach and apply it consistently so CI and developer machines cannot install an incompatible glob 11.1.x. In `@src/actions/integrations/intervals.ts`: - Line 84: The inline comment next to the object property bodyBattery is misleading (it says "Changed from d.bodyBattery to d.readiness" while the code still uses d.bodyBattery); either update the comment to accurately reflect the current code (e.g., remove the "Changed from..." text or replace with "// TODO: change to d.readiness" if you intend to switch later) or, if the intention was to use readiness, change the property assignment to bodyBattery: d.readiness and ensure any upstream callers/typing accept that change; locate the mapping that contains bodyBattery (in src/actions/integrations/intervals.ts) and make the appropriate single-line correction and run tests/typechecks. In `@tests/unit/services/planner.test.ts`: - Line 22: Remove the leftover debug console.log by deleting the statement console.log("Mock factory for auditorOrchestrator running") from the mock factory for auditorOrchestrator in the planner unit tests (tests/unit/services/planner.test.ts) so test output is clean; ensure no other stray console logging remains in the mock setup or related factory functions. ```
🧹 Nitpick comments (5)
package.json (1)
`158-158`: **`path-to-regexp`-override ändrad från `^6.3.0` till `>=6.3.0` — tillåter nu major version 7+.** `>=6.3.0` inkluderar alla framtida major-versioner (7.x, 8.x, etc.), vilket kan införa breaking changes. Överväg om `>=6.3.0 <8.0.0` eller liknande begränsning är mer lämplig.
test_error.txt (1)
`1-29`: **Testfellogg bör inte committas till repot.** Filer som `test_error.txt` är tillfälliga CI-artefakter och hör inte hemma i versionskontroll. Använd istället GitHub Actions-artefakter eller CI-dashboard för att lagra loggutdata. Observera: Felet (`auditReport` är `undefined` vid rad 131 i `planner.ts`) pekar på ett verkligt problem som åtgärdas i testmocken, men bör också hanteras defensivt i produktionskoden (se kommentar på `planner.ts`).
help_output.txt (1)
`1-39`: **CLI-hjälputdata bör inte committas till repot.** Filen `help_output.txt` är en tillfällig utdata från Gemini CLI och hör inte hemma i versionskontroll. Överväg att lägga till den i `.gitignore` om den genereras automatiskt.
tests/unit/services/planner.test.ts (2)
`21-33`: **Mock-strukturen ser korrekt ut – alias-sökvägen matchar produktionskoden.** Fabriksmocken returnerar ett rikt resultatobjekt med `highestPriorityGap`, `muscleAudits`, `ratios`, `overallScore` och `timestamp`, vilket troligen krävs för att `PlannerService.triggerWeeklyPlanGeneration` ska kunna köras utan fel. Dock verifieras ingen av dessa fält i testet (rad 76–78). Överväg att lägga till assertions som faktiskt validerar audit-resultatet om det påverkar den genererade planen, annars riskerar ni att regressioner i audit-logiken passerar obemärkt. --- `62-79`: **Testet täcker enbart "happy path" – inga negativa scenarion.** Testet verifierar att en plan genereras för en giltig användare, men saknar fall som: - Användare utan `hevyApiKey` / `intervalsApiKey` - `runFullAudit` som kastar ett fel - `OracleService.consult` som returnerar `null` Dessa edge cases bör täckas för att fånga regressioner i planner-logiken.
